figures.caption: Fig. 4. Step-wise performance trajectory. TestDecision exhibits a steeper growth curve compared to baselines.

Table 2. Main Results on ULT Benchmark. TestDecision significantly outperforms all open-source baselines, including advanced prompting strategies (ChatTester, SymPrompt) and learning-based methods (TestCTRL).

Fig. 3. The overview of TestDecision. The framework operates as an iterative generation loop. The LLM acts as a greedy policy generator, observing the code state augmented with checked markers. The environment executes the generated test, checks its correctness, and updates the state for the next step, ensuring the LLM always targets the remaining checked frontier.
Fig. 1. Illustrative example of the sequential dependency. Test C is functionally valid, but its contribution depends entirely on whether Tests A and B already exist in the suite.
Table 1. Pilot study results of Direct Generation, Blind Iteration, and Guided Iteration.

Fig. 2. Coverage growth with respect to test suite size (𝑘). All models exhibit a rapid plateau, characteristic of diminishing returns. Notably, the iter-guided lines do not distinctly separate from the iter-blind lines.
Table 5. Comprehensive Comparison with Proprietary Models, Different Scales, and Traditional Approaches.
